Message.Name: CHKMSG_BELONGS_MULTI_GROUP
Message.Value: A node can not belong to multiple target groups.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_CTRL_NODE_NOT_ROOT
Message.Value: Controller node must be the root node.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_DUPLICATE_LUN
Message.Value: LUN {0} duplicate.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_DUPLICATE_NETWORK_PORTAL_ID
Message.Value: Network portal ID {0} duplicate.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_DUPLICATE_TARGET_DEVICE_ID
Message.Value: Target device ID {0} duplicate.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_DUPLICATE_TARGET_GROUP_ID
Message.Value: Target group ID {0} duplicate.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_DUPLICATE_TARGET_ID
Message.Value: Target ID {0} duplicate.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_DUPLICATE_MEDIA_ID
Message.Value: Media ID {0} duplicate.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_DUPLICATE_TARGET_NAME
Message.Value: Target name {0} duplicate.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_BLOCKSIZE
Message.Value: Block size Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_CHAP_AUTH_PASSWORD_FORMAT
Message.Value: CHAP password format is invalid.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_CHAP_AUTH_PASSWORD_OMIT
Message.Value: If target authentication is omitted, the target password must be blank.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_CHAP_AUTH_USERNAME_FORMAT
Message.Value: CHAP user name format is invalid. Must be follow iSCSI text-Value format specification ( Reffer RFC 3720 5.1 Text format ).

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_DEFAULTTIME2RETAIN
Message.Value: DefaultTime2Retain Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_DEFAULTTIME2WAIT
Message.Value: DefaultTime2Wait Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_MEDIA_ID_VALUE
Message.Value: Media ID is invalid. Must not be zero.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_FILE_NAME_LENGTH
Message.Value: File name length is invalid. Must be greater thatn or equal 1 and less than or equal {0}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_FIRSTBURSTLENGTH
Message.Value: FirstBurstLength Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_LOGLEVEL
Message.Value: Invalid log level Value({0}).

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_LOGPARAM_HARDLIMIT
Message.Value: Log parameter hard limit Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_LOGPARAM_MAXFILECOUNT
Message.Value: Log parameter max file count Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_LOGPARAM_SOFTLIMIT
Message.Value: Log parameter soft limit Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_LOGPARAM_TOTALLIMIT
Message.Value: Log parameter total limit Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_LUN_CNT_IN_TARGET
Message.Value: Number of LUN in a target is invalid. Must be greater thatn or equal 1 and less than or equal {0}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_LUN_VALUE
Message.Value: LUN must not be zero.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_MAXBURSTLENGTH
Message.Value: MaxBurstLength Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_MAXMULTIPLICITY
Message.Value: Max multiplicity Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_MAXOUTSTANDINGR2T
Message.Value: MaxOutstandingR2T Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_MAXRECVDATASEGMENTLENGTH
Message.Value: MaxRecvDataSegmentLength Value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_PARENT_TYPE
Message.Value: {0} node can not be child of {1} node.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_PORT_NUMBER
Message.Value: Invalid iSCSI network portal TCP port number({0}).

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_RECEIVE_BUFFER_SIZE
Message.Value: ReceiveBufferSize must be greater than or equal 0.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_SEND_BUFFER_SIZE
Message.Value: SendBufferSize must be greater than or equal 0.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_IP_WHITELIST_TOO_LONG
Message.Value: Too many IP white list conditions count. Must be less than or equal {0}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_BLOCKSIZE
Message.Value: Block Size value {0} is out of range. Must be 512 or 4096.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_RELATION
Message.Value: Node {0} can not be child of node {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_REMOTE_CTRL_ADDRESS
Message.Value: Invalid remote control TCP port address({0}).

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_REMOTE_CTRL_PORT_NUM
Message.Value: Invalid remote control TCP port number({0}).

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_TARGET_NAME_FORMAT
Message.Value: Target name {0} is invalid. Must be follow iSCSI-name format specification ( Reffer RFC 3720 5.1 Text format ).

Message.Name: CHKMSG_LOGPARAM_HARDLIMIT_LESS_THAN_SOFTLIMIT
Message.Value: Log parameter hard limit Value {0} must be greater than or equal soft limit Value {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_LU_NAME_TOO_LONG
Message.Value: LU name name is too long. Must be less than or equal {0}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_LU_MAXMULTIPLICITY
Message.Value: LU max multiplicity value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_OPTIMAL_TRANSFER_LENGTH
Message.Value: Optimal Transfer Length value {0} is out of range. Must be {1} to {2}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_MISSING_LU
Message.Value: LU is missing. At least one LU node must be specified.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_MISSING_MEDIA
Message.Value: Media is missing. At least one media node must be specified.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_MISSING_NETWORK_PORTAL
Message.Value: Network portal is missing. At least one network portal node must be specified.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_MISSING_PARENT
Message.Value: {0} node can not be root node.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_ISOLATED_LU
Message.Value: Inaccessible LU exists.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_MISSING_TARGET
Message.Value: Target is missing. At least one target node must be specified.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_MISSING_TARGET_GROUP
Message.Value: Target group is missing. At least one target group node must be specified.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_OUT_OF_LU_COUNT_IN_TD
Message.Value: Number of LU in a target device exceed the limit. Must be less than or equal {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_OUT_OF_LU_COUNT_IN_TG
Message.Value: Number of LU in a target group exceed the limit. Must be less than or equal {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_OUT_OF_MEDIA_COUNT
Message.Value: Number of media exceed the limit. Must be less than or equal {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_OUT_OF_NETWORK_PORTAL_COUNT
Message.Value: Number of network portal in a target device exceed the limit. Must be less than or equal {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_OUT_OF_TARGET_COUNT_IN_TD
Message.Value: Number of target in a target device exceed the limit. Must be less than or equal {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_OUT_OF_TARGET_COUNT_IN_TG
Message.Value: Number of target in a target group exceed the limit. Must be less than or equal {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_OUT_OF_TARGET_DEVICE_COUNT
Message.Value: Number of target devices exceed the limit. Must be less than or equal {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_OUT_OF_TARGET_GROUP_COUNT
Message.Value: Number of target group exceed the limit. Must be less than or equal {1}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_TARGET_ADDRESS_TOO_LONG
Message.Value: Target address string is too long. Must be less than or equal {0}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_TARGET_ALIAS_TOO_LONG
Message.Value: Target alias string is too long. Must be less than or equal {0}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_TARGET_DEVICE_NAME_TOO_LONG
Message.Value: Target device name is too long. Must be less than or equal {0}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_TARGET_GROUP_NAME_TOO_LONG
Message.Value: Target group name is too long. Must be less than or equal {0}.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_TOO_MANY_PARENT
Message.Value: {0} node can not have more than {1} parents.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_TOO_MANY_CHILD
Message.Value: {0} node can not have more than {1} child nodes.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_TOO_FEW_CHILD
Message.Value: {0} node must have at least {1} child nodes.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_UNSUPPORTED_TPGT_VALUE
Message.Value: TPGT must be zero.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_UNSUPPORTED_TARGET_NODE_ID_VALUE
Message.Value: Target node ID must not be zero.

Message.Name: CHKMSG_INVALID_MEDIA_SIZE
Message.Value: Too large media size. Max value is {0}

Message.Name: EmptyAddress
Message.Value: Address is not specified.

Message.Name: EmptyPort
Message.Value: Port is not specified.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_FAILED_CONNECT
Message.Value: Failed to connect to the controller.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_FAILED_IDENT_TARGET_DEVICE
Message.Value: Unable to identify target device node which specified node ({0}) belongs to.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_FAILED_IDENT_TARGET_GROUP
Message.Value: Unable to identify target group node which specified node ({0}) belongs to.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_FAILED_LOGOUT
Message.Value: Failed to logout.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_FAILED_NO_OPERATION
Message.Value: Failed to send no operation request.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_FAILED_SET_LOGPARAM
Message.Value: Failed to set log parameters.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_FAILED_TERMINATE_TARGET_DEVICE
Message.Value: Failed to terminate target device.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_NODE_IS_NOT_TARGET_DEVICE
Message.Value: Specified node ID({0}) is not target device.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_DEVICE_MODIFIED
Message.Value: The target device has been modified.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_DEVICE_PROC_RUNNING
Message.Value: Target device process is running yet.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_DEVICE_RUNNING
Message.Value: Target device process is still running.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_DEVICE_NOT_RUNNING
Message.Value: Target device process is not running.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_GROUP_LOADED
Message.Value: Specified target group is loaded.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_GROUP_MODIFIED
Message.Value: The target group has been modified.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_GROUP_UNLOADED
Message.Value: Specified target group is unloaded.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_GROUP_ACTIVATED
Message.Value: Specified target group is activated.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TARGET_GROUP_INACTIVATED
Message.Value: Specified target group is inactivated.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_UNEXPECTED_RESPONSE
Message.Value: Failed to send {0} request. Unexpected response was received.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_UNEXPEDTED_CONFIGURATION_ERROR
Message.Value: An unexpected configuration error was detected.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_VALIDATION_FAILED
Message.Value: A configuration error remains. Eliminate any configuration errors.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TEMP_EXPORT_VERSION_MISMATCH
Message.Value: Temp export data version mismatch.

Message.Name: ERRMSG_TEMP_EXPORT_MISSING_ROOT
Message.Value: Temp export data has no root node.

Message.Name: FailedToConnect
Message.Value: Failed to connect server.

Message.Name: InvalidPortNumber
Message.Value: Invalid port number.

Message.Name: CMDERR_UNKNOWN_COMMAND
Message.Value: '{0}' is unknown.

Message.Name: CMDERR_LAST_ARG_VAL_MISSING
Message.Value: Missing value of the last argument.

Message.Name: CMDERR_INVALID_ARG_COUNT
Message.Value: Invalid arguments count.

Message.Name: CMDERR_INVALID_ARG_VALUE
Message.Value: Invalid value '{0}' was specified.

Message.Name: CMDERR_NAMELESS_PTN_MISMATCH
Message.Value: Invalid argument values

Message.Name: CMDERR_FAILED_CONNECT_CTRL
Message.Value: Failed connect to controller. {0}

Message.Name: CMDERR_CONNECTION_ERROR
Message.Value: Connection error. {0}

Message.Name: CMDERR_FAILED_LOGIN
Message.Value: Failed login to controller. {0}

Message.Name: CMDERR_UNEXPECTED_REQUEST_ERROR
Message.Value: Unexpected request error. {0}

Message.Name: CMDERR_UNEXPECTED_EDIT_ERROR
Message.Value: Unexpected error. {0}

Message.Name: CMDMSG_MISSING_NODE
Message.Value: Specified node({0}) does not exist.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_ADDPARAM_LUN
Message.Value: LUN(/l) must be specified.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_ADDPARAM_MISSING_LUN
Message.Value: Specified LUN does not exist.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_ADDPARAM_MEDIA_TYPE
Message.Value: Unknown Media type.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_CONFIG_MODIFIED
Message.Value: Configuration is modified but not pulished.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_ADDPARAM_LOGIN_PORT
Message.Value: Port number():

Message.Name: CMDMSG_MISSING_CHILD_NODE
Message.Value: There are no child nodes.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_MISSING_PARENT_NODE
Message.Value: There are no parent nodes.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_UNKNOWN_PARAMETER_NAME
Message.Value: Unknown value name was specified. Acceptable name is {0}.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_PARAMVAL_DATATYPE_MISMATCH
Message.Value: The specified parameter values have mismatched data types. The expected type is {0}.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_PARAMVAL_INVALID_PARAM_PATTERN
Message.Value: Invalid parameter specification.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_MISSING_CONFIGURABLE_VALUE
Message.Value: Current node has no configurable parameter values.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_ALL_VALIDATED
Message.Value: All configurations are vlidated.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_CTRL_NODE_NOT_DELETABLE
Message.Value: Controller node can not be delete.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_CONFIGURATION_PUBLISHED
Message.Value: All configurations are uploaded to the controller.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_LOG_PARAM_UPDATED
Message.Value: Log parameter updated.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_TOO_MANY_CHILD
Message.Value: Too many child nodes, can't add any more.

Message.Name: CMDMSG_HELP_UNKNOWN_CMD
Message.Value: Unknown command.

Message.Name: EXIT
Message.Value: exit : Terminate client command.

Message.Name: HELP
Message.Value: help : Display help message.

Message.Name: LOGIN
Message.Value: login : Connect to the controller.

Message.Name: LOGOUT
Message.Value: logout : Disconnect from the controller.

Message.Name: RELOAD
Message.Value: reload : Discard all changes and reload the configurations.

Message.Name: SELECT
Message.Value: select : Select the child object as the target for operation or modification.

Message.Name: UNSELECT
Message.Value: unselect : Select the parent object as the target for operation or modification.

Message.Name: LIST
Message.Value: list : Display a list of child objects.

Message.Name: LISTPARENT
Message.Value: listparent : Display a list of parent objects.

Message.Name: PWD
Message.Value: pwd : Displays the currently selected object.

Message.Name: VALUES
Message.Value: values : Displays a list of settings for the currently selected object.

Message.Name: SET
Message.Value: set : Changes the settings of the currently selected object.

Message.Name: VALIDATE
Message.Value: validate : Verify that there are no errors in the configuration or settings.

Message.Name: PUBLISH
Message.Value: publish : Upload the changed configuration to the controller.

Message.Name: NOP
Message.Value: nop : No operation

Message.Name: STATUSALL
Message.Value: statusall : Displays the current running, stopped, and editing status.

Message.Name: CREATE_TARGET_DEVICE
Message.Value: create : Add Target Device as a child object.

Message.Name: STATUS
Message.Value: status : Displays the current running, stopped, and editing status.

Message.Name: DELETE
Message.Value: delete : Delete object.

Message.Name: START
Message.Value: start : Start the target device.

Message.Name: KILL
Message.Value: kill : Stop the target device.

Message.Name: SETLOGPARAM
Message.Value: setlogparam : Temporarily change log output settings.

Message.Name: GETLOGPARAM
Message.Value: getlogparam : Display the currently effective log output settings.

Message.Name: CREATE_NETWORKPORTAL
Message.Value: create networkportal : Add Network Portal as a child object.

Message.Name: CREATE_TARGETGROUP
Message.Value: create targetgroup : Add Target Group as a child object.

Message.Name: ADD_IPWHITELIST
Message.Value: add ipwhitelist : Add the source IP address to the whitelist.

Message.Name: CLEAR_IPWHITELIST
Message.Value: clear ipwhitelist : Delete all IP whitelist entries.

Message.Name: LOAD
Message.Value: load : Load the Target Group.

Message.Name: UNLOAD
Message.Value: unload : Unload the Target Group.

Message.Name: ACTIVATE
Message.Value: activate : Enables access to LUs belonging to the Target Group.

Message.Name: INACTIVATE
Message.Value: inactivate : Prohibits access to LUs belonging to the Target Group.

Message.Name: CREATE_TARGET
Message.Value: create : Add Target as a child object.

Message.Name: SETCHAP
Message.Value: setchap : Add CHAP authentication.

Message.Name: UNSETAUTH
Message.Value: unsetauth : Remove authentication settings.

Message.Name: CREATE_LU
Message.Value: create : Add LU as a child object.

Message.Name: ATTACH
Message.Value: attach : Connect the Target and the LU.

Message.Name: DETACH
Message.Value: detach : Disconnect the connection between the Target and the LU.

Message.Name: CREATE_PLAINFILE
Message.Value: create plainfile : Add Plain File Media as a child object.

Message.Name: CREATE_MEMBUFFER
Message.Value: create membuffer : Add Memory Buffer Media as a child object.

Message.Name: CREATE_DEBUG
Message.Value: create debug : Add Debug Media as a child object.

Message.Name: INITMEDIA_PLAINFILE
Message.Value: initmedia plainfile : Create a media file for Plain File Media.

Message.Name: IMSTATUS
Message.Value: imstatus : Displays the progress of the media file creation process.

Message.Name: IMKILL
Message.Value: imkill : Stop the media file creation process.

Message.Name: SESSIONS
Message.Value: sessions : Display a list of sessions.

Message.Name: SESSKILL
Message.Value: sesskill : Disconnect the session.

Message.Name: CONNECTIONS
Message.Value: connections : Display a list of connections.

Message.Name: LUSTATUS
Message.Value: lustatus : Display LU status.

Message.Name: LURESET
Message.Value: lureset : Perform LU reset.

Message.Name: MEDIASTATUS
Message.Value: mediastatus : Display media status.

Message.Name: ADD_TRAP
Message.Value: add trap : Add a trap to Debug Media.

Message.Name: CLEAR_TRAP
Message.Value: clear trap : Remove the Debug Media traps.

Message.Name: TRAPS
Message.Value: traps : Display a list of traps configured in Debug Media.

Message.Name: TASK_LIST
Message.Value: task list : Display a list of ITTs that are currently pending due to the Wait action.

Message.Name: TASK_RESUME
Message.Value: task resume : Resume pending tasks.

Message.Name: EXIT
Message.Value:
exit [/y]
/y If the configuration information has been changed, discard the changes and terminate the client command.

Message.Name: HELP
Message.Value:
help [command]
Displays context-dependent help messages. If no command name is specified, a list of currently available command names is displayed. If a command name is specified, detailed usage instructions for the specified command are displayed.

Message.Name: LOGIN
Message.Value:
login /h host /p port [/f]
/h host Specify the hostname or IP address of the destination controller. /p port Specify the TCP port number of the destination controller. /f Discard the session of the other client.
Connects to the controller listening at the specified IP address and TCP port number. If another client is already logged in, the login will fail. If the "/f" option is specified, the login from this client will be given priority, and the sessions of other clients will be discarded.

Message.Name: LOGOUT
Message.Value:
logout [/y]
/y If the configuration information has been changed, discard the changes and disconnect from the controller.

Message.Name: RELOAD
Message.Value:
reload [/y]
/y If the configuration information has been changed, discard the changes and reload the configurations.

Message.Name: SELECT
Message.Value:
select index
index Specify the index number of the child object to select. The index number can be displayed using the list command.

Message.Name: UNSELECT
Message.Value:
unselect [/p index]
/p Specify the index number of the parent object to select. The index number can be displayed using the listparent command. If omitted, 0 is assumed.
This command selects the parent object specified in the argument. Note that if the currently selected object has multiple parents, it does not "return" to the object before the current object was selected.

Message.Name: LIST
Message.Value:
list
This command displays a list of child objects registered to the currently selected object. The index value is used as an argument to the select command.

Message.Name: LISTPARENT
Message.Value:
listparent
This command displays a list of parent objects registered to the currently selected object. The index value is used as an argument to the unselect command.

Message.Name: PWD
Message.Value:
pwd
This command displays a short description of the currently selected object. It displays the same information as the list and listparent commands.

Message.Name: VALUES
Message.Value:
values
This command displays detailed information about the currently selected object, including its settings. The settings include the item name, data type, and the set value.

Message.Name: SET
Message.Value:
set name value
name Specify the name of the setting item whose value should be changed. Case sensitivity is ignored. values Specify the value to be set.

Message.Name: VALIDATE
Message.Value:
validate
Verify that there are no errors in the modified configuration. All errors must be removed before uploading the configuration information to the controller. Note that the validate command is implicitly executed when the publish command is run.

Message.Name: PUBLISH
Message.Value:
publish
Upload the modified configuration information to the controller. The modified configuration information will not take effect unless it is uploaded to the controller by executing this command. The validate command is also implicitly executed when this command is run. All errors must be removed before uploading the configuration information.

Message.Name: NOP
Message.Value:
nop
This command issues a "No Operation" command to the controller. Executing this command extends the session timeout value. This command can also be used to check the controller's health and maintain the TCP connection.

Message.Name: STATUSALL
Message.Value:
statusall
This command displays the operational status and any changes to all registered controllers, target devices, and target groups.

Message.Name: CREATE_TARGET_DEVICE
Message.Value:
create [/n name]
/n Specify the name of the newly created target device.
A Target Device will be created and registered as a child of the currently selected object. All settings except the name will be created with their default values. Note that default values ​​are not always practical; therefore, settings must be changed as needed.

Message.Name: STATUS
Message.Value:
status
This command displays the operational status and any changes to the controller, Target Device, and Target Group to which the currently selected object belongs.

Message.Name: DELETE
Message.Value:
delete [/i index]
/i Specify the index value of the child object to delete.
If an index value is specified with /i, the selected child object is deleted. If the argument is omitted, the currently selected object is deleted and the parent object is selected. If there are multiple parent objects, index 0 is treated as specified.

Message.Name: START
Message.Value:
start
This command launches the Target Device to which the currently selected object belongs.

Message.Name: KILL
Message.Value:
kill
This command stop the Target Device to which the currently selected object belongs. All LUs belonging to the Target Device will also be shut down.

Message.Name: SETLOGPARAM
Message.Value:
setlogparam [/s SoftLimit] [/h HardLimit] [/l Level]
/s Specify a soft limit for the number of log lines output per second. /h Specify a hard limit for the number of log lines output per second. /l Specify one of the following: VERBOSE, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, FAILED, or OFF.
This command temporarily changes the log output limit and output level. The changes are effective while the Target Device is running, but will be reset to the values ​​set in the configuration information upon reboot. Note that this command cannot be used to change configuration information.

Message.Name: GETLOGPARAM
Message.Value:
getlogparam
This command displays the currently active log output limit and log output level.

Message.Name: CREATE_NETWORKPORTAL
Message.Value:
create networkportal [/a address] [/p port]
/a Specify the IP address or hostname to listen for incoming calls from the initiator. /p Specifies the TCP port number to listen for incoming calls from the initiator.
Add an iSCSI Network Portal. If no IP address or hostname is specified, the system will listen for incoming calls on all available ports. If the port number is omitted, the default value of 3260 is used. The validate and publish commands do not verify the validity of addresses or port numbers. It is the user's responsibility to specify available addresses and ports.

Message.Name: CREATE_TARGETGROUP
Message.Value:
create targetgroup [/n name]
/n Specify the name of the newly created target group.
A Target Device will be created and registered as a child of the currently selected object. All settings except the name will be created with their default values. Note that default values ​​are not always practical; therefore, settings must be changed as needed.

Message.Name: ADD_IPWHITELIST
Message.Value:
add ipwhitelist [/fadr address] [/fmask mask] [/t type]
/fabr Specify the IP address. /fmask Specify the mask value to be applied to the IP address. /t Specify the types of IP addresses that are allowed to receive incoming connections. It must be one of the following: ANY, LOOPBACK, LINKLOCAL, PRIVATE, MULTICAST, GLOBAL, IPV4ANY, IPV4LOOPBACK, IPV4LINKLOCAL, IPv4PRIVATE, IPV4MULTICAST, IPV4GLOBAL, IPV6ANY, IPV6LOOPBACK, IPV6LINKLOCAL, IPV6PRIVATE, IPV6MULTICAST, IPV6GLOBAL.
Add the IP addresses of incoming connections to the whitelist. Either both "/fabr" and "/fmask", or "/t" alone must be specified.
When a TCP connection is established, the IP whitelist compares each entry in the order they were registered, and if a match is found, the incoming connection is permitted. If there is no entry registered in the IP whitelist, all incoming connections will be allowed.
If "/fabr 192.168.111.222" and "/fmask 255.255.0.0" are specified, incoming TCP connections with source IP addresses between 192.168.0.0 and 192.168.255.255 will be allowed. Both "/fabr" and "/fmask" can be used with IPv4 and IPv6, but the same type must be used for both.

Message.Name: CLEAR_IPWHITELIST
Message.Value:
clear ipwhitelist
Remove all entries from the list of allowed incoming IP addresses. Note that if no entries are registered, all incoming calls will be allowed.

Message.Name: LOAD
Message.Value:
load
Load the configuration information of the Target Group to which the currently selected object belongs and make it ready for execution. Note that incoming connections from the initiator will not be allowed until the "activate" command is executed.

Message.Name: UNLOAD
Message.Value:
unload
Stop execution and unload configuration information for the currently selected Target Group. To modify the configuration information of objects belonging to a Target Group, such as Targets and LUs etc, the Target Group must be unloaded beforehand.

Message.Name: ACTIVATE
Message.Value:
activate
Allow the initiator to connect to the Target Group to which the currently selected object belongs.

Message.Name: INACTIVATE
Message.Value:
inactivate
This command prohibits initiators from connecting to the Target Group to which the currently selected object belongs. Executing this command will prevent new connections. Note that existing sessions will remain intact.

Message.Name: CREATE_TARGET
Message.Value:
create [/n name]
/n Specify the iSCSI target name.
A Target will be created and registered as a child of the currently selected object. All settings except the name will be created with their default values. Note that default values ​​are not always practical; therefore, settings must be changed as needed.

Message.Name: SETCHAP
Message.Value:
setchap /iu user /ip password [/tu user] [/tp password]
/iu Specify the username to be used for initiator authentication. /ip Specify the password to be used for initiator authentication. /tu Specify the username to be used for target authentication. /tp Specify the password to be used for target authentication.
Specify the username and password to be used for CHAP authentication. If authentication information is not registered, the connection will be permitted without authentication.

Message.Name: UNSETAUTH
Message.Value:
unsetauth
Delete all registered authentication information. If authentication information is not registered, the connection will be permitted without authentication.

Message.Name: CREATE_LU
Message.Value:
create /l LUN [/n name]
/l Specify the LUN. /n Specify the LU name.
A LU will be created and registered as a child of the currently selected object. All settings except the name and LUN will be created with their default values. Note that default values ​​are not always practical; therefore, settings must be changed as needed.
The LUN must be a value between 1 and 255. Furthermore, it cannot be duplicated within a single Target Device.

Message.Name: ATTACH
Message.Value:
attach LUN
LUN Specify the LUN.
This configures the currently selected Target to be able to access the LU of the specified LUN. Note that an attached LU will have multiple parents.

Message.Name: DETACH
Message.Value:
detach LUN
LUN Specify the LUN.
Unregister LU, who is registered as a child of target. If a specified LU becomes inaccessible from all Targets, that LU is deleted.

Message.Name: CREATE_PLAINFILE
Message.Value:
create plainfile FileName
FileName Specify the file name to access.
Register an object for accessing RAW files (regular files without a specific format) as a child of the currently selected object. All settings except the file name will be created with their default values. Note that default values ​​are not always practical; therefore, settings must be changed as needed.
The path name is represented by the path name in the system where the controller or Target Device is running. Note that this will differ from the path name on the client's system if the client is connected to a remote controller. If a file name is specified using a relative path, it will be interpreted as a relative path from the configuration directory specified when the controller was started. To avoid confusion, it is recommended to specify an absolute path.

Message.Name: CREATE_MEMBUFFER
Message.Value:
create membuffer size
size Specify the media size in bytes.
Registers an object that uses a memory buffer as its medium as a child of the currently selected object. All settings except the media size will be created with their default values. Note that default values ​​are not always practical; therefore, settings must be changed as needed.

Message.Name: CREATE_DEBUG
Message.Value:
create debug
Register the object that provides debugging functionality as a child of the currently selected object.

Message.Name: INITMEDIA_PLAINFILE
Message.Value:
initmedia plainfile FileName Size
FileName Specify the path name of the file to be created. Size Specify the file size of the file to be created in bytes.
Create a file to be used with the Plain File Media. The path name is represented by the path name in the system where the controller or Target Device is running. Note that this will differ from the path name on the client's system if the client is connected to a remote controller. If a file name is specified using a relative path, it will be interpreted as a relative path from the configuration directory specified when the controller was started. To avoid confusion, it is recommended to specify an absolute path.

Message.Name: IMSTATUS
Message.Value:
imstatus
Displays the progress of the file creation process.

Message.Name: IMKILL
Message.Value:
imkill index
index Specify the index of the file creation process that should be stopped.
The index value is displayed using the imstatus command.

Message.Name: SESSIONS
Message.Value:
sessions
Display a list of currently active sessions.

Message.Name: SESSKILL
Message.Value:
sesskill TSIH
TSIH Specify the TSIH of the session to be disconnected.
The specified session will be forcibly terminated.

Message.Name: CONNECTIONS
Message.Value:
connections [/s TSIH]
/s Specify the TSIH of the session for which the list of connections should be displayed.
Displays a list of currently connected connections. If TSIH is specified, a list of connections belonging to the specified session is displayed. If TSIH is omitted, it displays a list of connections within the session related to the Target Device, Network Portal, Target Group, and Target, depending on the currently selected object.

Message.Name: LUSTATUS
Message.Value:
lustatus
Displays the status and operational status of the LU.

Message.Name: LURESET
Message.Value:
lureset
Perform an LU reset. Running tasks will be aborted, and media will be closed and reloaded. Note that all contents of Memory Buffer Media will be erased.

Message.Name: MEDIASTATUS
Message.Value:
mediastatus
Displays the status and operational status of the media.

Message.Name: ADD_TRAP
Message.Value:
add trap [/e event] [/slba LBA] [/elba LBA] [/a action] [/msg message] [/idx index] [/ms MiliSec]
/e Specify the event that will trigger the Trap. Choose one of the following: TestUnitReady, ReadCapacity, Read, Write, or Format. /slba In a trap triggered by a Read or Write command, specify the starting position of the access range that triggers the trap. /elba In a trap triggered by a Read or Write command, specify the end position of the access range that triggers the trap. /a Specify the action to be performed by the Trap. Specify one of the following: ACA, LUReset, Count, Delay, or Wait. /msg Specify the message that is output when performing an ACA or LU reset. /idx Specify the index value of the counter to which the value will be added. /ms Specify the waiting time in milliseconds when delaying task processing using the Delay action.
Register various Traps and Actions to be used for debugging. Traps can only be registered for active Debug Media. Registered traps are cleared when an LU reset, Target Group unload, or Target Device is restarted.
Traps are executed in the order they are registered. For example, if a trap that triggers ACA is registered first, subsequent traps will not be executed.

Message.Name: CLEAR_TRAP
Message.Value:
clear trap
Delete all Traps currently registered in the selected Debug Media.

Message.Name: TRAPS
Message.Value:
traps
This command displays a list of traps registered to the currently selected Debug Media.

Message.Name: TASK_LIST
Message.Value:
task list
This command displays a list of tasks that are waiting to run due to a Trap in Debug Media that performs a Wait action. Tasks stopped by the Wait action will not resume execution until restarted by the task resume command.

Message.Name: TASK_RESUME
Message.Value:
task resume tsih itt
tsih Specify the session to which the task that should be resumed belongs. itt Specify the initiator task tag of the task that should be resumed.
This command resumes a task that has been stopped by a Trap in Debug Media that performs a Wait action.
